meta.description: Production EU/UK → US ADR latency watchlist with feeds
meta.strategy: home_market_news → ADR/OTC lag. Run feeds continuously during US hours (09:30-16:00 EST). Edge exists whenever home feed publishes AND US OTC is open, regardless of whether home market is open or closed. Post-close window (home closed, US open) = largest mispricing.

LSE_RNS.description: London Stock Exchange RNS — search by TIDM (home_ticker)
LSE_RNS.dst_note: UK (BST) and US (EDT) change clocks at similar times — net offset stays 5 hrs year-round

OSLO_BORS.description: Oslo Stock Exchange Newsweb — issuer_id search
OSLO_BORS.dst_note: Norway follows CET/CEST — 10:20 stable year-round. Overlap is only 50 minutes; effectively a post-close feed

EURONEXT.description: Euronext — Paris, Amsterdam, Brussels, Lisbon — ISIN + MIC search
EURONEXT.dst_note: CET/CEST and EST/EDT change together — 11:30 is stable year-round. Exception: ~2-week DST gap in March/October where close shifts to 12:30 or 10:30 EDT

XETRA.description: Deutsche Boerse Xetra — DGAP ad-hoc announcements — ISIN search
XETRA.dst_note: CET/CEST and EST/EDT change together — 11:30 stable. ~3-week DST gap spring/autumn shifts ±1 hr

SIX.dst_note: Switzerland follows CET/CEST — same as Germany

NASDAQ_NORDIC.description: Nasdaq Nordic — Stockholm (XSTO), Copenhagen (XCSE), Helsinki (XHEL) — instrument_id search
NASDAQ_NORDIC.dst_note: Stockholm/Helsinki 11:30 EST; Copenhagen closes slightly earlier (17:00 CET = 11:00 EST) but treated as 11:30 for simplicity

CNMV.description: CNMV Spain — hechos relevantes RSS, filter by ISIN
CNMV.dst_note: Spain follows CET/CEST — same as Germany/France

TSE.description: Tokyo Stock Exchange TDnet — tse_code (4-digit) search
TSE.dst_note: Japan has no DST. Home always closed before US opens. Full 6h30m session is home-closed. Filings in Japanese — primary source of information asymmetry edge

KRX.description: Korea Exchange DART — dart_corp_code search. Requires DART_API_KEY env var
KRX.dst_note: Korea has no DST. Home always closed before US opens. DART free API key required from dart.fss.or.kr. Filings in Korean

HKEX.description: Hong Kong Stock Exchange — hkex_stock_code search
HKEX.dst_note: Hong Kong has no DST. Home always closed before US opens. English filings available

ASX.description: Australian Securities Exchange — asx_code JSON API search
ASX.dst_note: Australia observes DST Oct–Apr (AEDT=UTC+11), but this is US winter, so offsets roughly cancel. Home always closes ~01:00 EST regardless of season. English filings

NSE.home_exchange: National Stock Exchange of India / BSE
NSE.dst_note: India has no DST (IST = UTC+5:30 always). Home always closed well before US opens. English filings

JSE.dst_note: SAST = UTC+2 always (South Africa has no DST). US clock changes cause 1-hour seasonal shift. Winter: home closes 10:00 EST (6h post-close). Summer: home closes 11:00 EDT (5h post-close)

TASE.description: Tel Aviv Stock Exchange MAYA — tase_company_id search
TASE.dst_note: Israel DST (IDT=UTC+3) roughly aligns with US DST so offset stays ~10:30 EST year-round. TRADES SUNDAY–THURSDAY ONLY — no Friday signals

B3.description: Brazil B3/CVM — fatos relevantes RSS, filter by CVM code or ISIN. Simultaneous with US market. Edge from language/attention gap only.
B3.dst_note: Brazil abolished DST in 2019 — BRT=UTC-3 always. US clock changes cause a seasonal shift: winter gives a 30-minute post-close window (15:30–16:00 EST); summer B3 closes after US (16:30 EDT) so home is never closed during US session. Edge is primarily language and attention gap, not structural mispricing from closed home market. Filings in Portuguese.

BMV.description: Mexico BMV EMISNET — eventos relevantes, search by bmv_ticker. Simultaneous with US market all year. Edge from language/attention gap only.
BMV.dst_note: Mexico observes CDT in summer (UTC-5), CST in winter (UTC-6), tracking closely with US Eastern. BMV closes at exactly 16:00 US Eastern year-round — simultaneous with US close, zero post-close window. Edge is purely language and attention gap. Filings in Spanish.
